perturbation.original_text: This Attachment and its Product Attachments shall become effective on the Effective Date and shall continue for a period of three (3) years unless terminated as provided in Section 5.0 of the Base Agreement. This Attachment will automatically be renewed for periods of six (6) months unless either party gives twelve (12) months written notice of its intent to terminate this Agreement. Such renewals shall continue for successive periods under the same terms and conditions, unless otherwise agreed in writing by both Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Attachment and its Product Attachments shall become effective on the Effective Date and shall continue for a period of three (3) years unless terminated as provided in Section 5.0 of the Base Agreement. This Attachment will automatically be renewed for periods of twelve (12) months unless either party gives six (6) months written notice of its intent to terminate this Agreement. Such renewals shall continue for successive periods under different terms and conditions, unless otherwise agreed in writing by both Parties.
perturbation.explanation: The renewal period and notice period are swapped, creating conflicting terms. Also, the phrase "same terms and conditions" is replaced with "different terms and conditions", which is self-contradictory and unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Common Law Principles of Contract Interpretation
perturbation.law_explanation: This modification introduces ambiguity and internal inconsistency, conflicting with contract interpretation principles. Contract law generally strives to give effect to the parties' intent, but here the intent is made unclear.

perturbation.justification: The original text specifies automatic renewal under the 'same terms and conditions,' ensuring consistency and predictability. The changed text alters this by stating renewals occur under 'different terms and conditions,' introducing inherent contradiction and unenforceability as it lacks specific details or mutual agreement on these changed terms. Furthermore, it swaps the renewal period and notice period. This creates ambiguity and directly contradicts the principle of clear and consistent agreement in contract law, as it is difficult to ascertain the actual intent and obligations of the parties.

perturbation.original_text: All of the above described warranty periods will commence on the date that the Products containing the above Parts are delivered to IBM. The above warranties shall survive acceptance test, and IBM's Product test procedure. MSL's warranty described above also covers latent defects resulting from MSL's specification, workmanship, process, and/or Parts purchased from MSL's suppliers and IBM's Nominated Suppliers.
perturbation.changed_text: All of the above described warranty periods will commence 30 days after the date that the Products containing the above Parts are delivered to IBM. The above warranties shall terminate upon acceptance test, regardless of IBM's Product test procedure. MSL's warranty described above only covers patent defects resulting from MSL's willful misconduct, workmanship, process, and/or Parts purchased from MSL's suppliers and IBM's Nominated Suppliers.
perturbation.explanation: The commencement of warranty is delayed, the survival clause is removed, and the defect coverage is restricted. Delaying the start of the warranty period to 30 days, specifying warranties end upon acceptance, and restricting the warranty to cover patent defects stemming from willful misconduct significantly limits IBM's protection.
perturbation.law_explanation: Modifying the warranty to only cover 'patent defects resulting from MSL's willful misconduct' attempts to disclaim implied warranties beyond what's legally permissible, especially concerning good faith and fair dealing, which the UCC implies.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-316. Exclusion or Modification of Warranties. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-316. Exclusion or Modification of Warranties. (1) Words or conduct relevant to the creation of an express warranty and words or conduct tending to negate or limit warranty shall be construed wherever reasonable as consistent with each other; but subject to the provisions of this Article on parol or extrinsic evidence (Section 2-202 ) negation or limitation is inoperative to the extent that such construction is unreasonable. (2)Subject to subsection (3), to exclude or modify the implied warranty of merchantability or any part of it the language must mention merchantability and in case of a writing must be conspicuous, and to exclude or modify any implied warranty of fitness the exclusion must be by a writing and conspicuous. Language to exclude all implied warranties of fitness is sufficient if it states, for example, that "There are no warranties which extend beyond the description on the face hereof." (3)Notwithstanding subsection (2) (a) unless the circumstances indicate otherwise, all implied warranties are excluded by expressions like "as is", "with all faults" or other language which in common understanding calls the buyer's attention to the exclusion of warranties and makes plain that there is no implied warranty; and (b) when the buyer before entering into the contract has examined the goods or the sample or model as fully as he desired or has refused to examine the goods there is no implied warranty with regard to defects which an examination ought in the circumstances to have revealed to him; and (c) an implied warranty can also be excluded or modified by course of dealing or course of performance or usage of trade. (4) Remedies for breach of warranty can be limited in accordance with the provisions of this Article on liquidation or limitation of damages and on contractual modification of remed
perturbation.justification: The modified text significantly restricts the warranty coverage, shifting from latent defects to only patent defects resulting from willful misconduct. The Law Explanation states this attempts to disclaim implied warranties beyond what's legally permissible under the UCC. Scraped Law Snippet 1, UCC § 2-316, addresses the exclusion or modification of warranties, stating that language must be conspicuous to exclude implied warranties. By narrowing the warranty scope to only 'patent defects resulting from MSL's willful misconduct,' the changed text introduces a significant limitation that could be construed as an attempt to disclaim implied warranties without meeting the conspicuousness requirement of UCC § 2-316, creating a contradiction. The original text's broader coverage of latent defects is effectively removed, diminishing the protection afforded to IBM.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement does not require either party to disclose or to receive Information. Neither party may assign, or otherwise transfer, its rights or delegate its duties or obligations under this Agreement without prior written consent. Any attempt to do so is void.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement requires either party to disclose or to receive Information when requested. Either party may assign, or otherwise transfer, its rights or delegate its duties or obligations under this Agreement without prior written consent. Any attempt to do so is valid.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the 'prior written consent' clause and stating any attempt to assign rights is valid contradicts standard contract law principles. The original clause ensures the agreement is only transferred to suitable parties and protects the interests of both. The added phrasing could violate confidentiality and operational control.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Common Law Principles of Contract Assignment
perturbation.law_explanation: By removing the requirement for prior written consent for assignment and delegation, the modified text contradicts established principles of contract law regarding the assignability of rights and delegation of duties. Certain contracts involving personal services or those where assignment would materially alter the obligations of the other party are generally not assignable without consent.

perturbation.justification: The modified text removes the 'prior written consent' requirement for assigning rights and delegates duties, and it declares that any attempt to do so is valid. This directly contradicts standard contract law principles, as it allows for the transfer of the agreement to potentially unsuitable parties without the original parties' consent. The absence of scraped law snippets doesn't negate the contradiction, as the Law Explanation clearly states that contracts involving personal services or those where assignment would materially alter the obligations of the other party are generally not assignable without consent. By making assignments valid without prior consent, the modified text undermines the original agreement's protections and operational control.
